program_name: Total central government spending on Science and Technology
description: Total central government spending on Science and Technology in 1997-98 is expected to be about £6 billion.

description: £20 million in 1997-98 for a challenge fund to finance scientific equipment.
University Challenge Fund to create seed venture capital funds to help commercially exploit universities' research.

set up the £50 million University Challenge fund to provide seed funding for commercialising research.
run the Science Enterprise Challenge. This provided £25 million for new centres to encourage enterprise in and around universities.

the £187 million Higher Education Innovation Fund (HEIF). In the next round of the HEIF, for investment between 2004 and 2006.

program_name: NHS R&D combined budget target (MRC + NHS)

a £150 million National Technology Strategy to provide a framework for policy priorities and focus support on innovation.

program_name: R&D tax credit extensions (software, power, water, fuel)

The next round of the Science Research Infrastructure Fund, which provides capital funding of £500 million per annum to universities.

program_name: Total UK science spending (2004 SR period)
description: by 2007-08 total UK science spending will be £5.4 billion representing an average annual growth of 5.8 per cent in real terms since 2004-05.

create a single, jointly held health research fund of at least £1 billion per year.
announcing a further £50 million in 2006-07 and £50 million in 2007-08 for the Enterprise Capital Funds scheme.
